yaml.title: The Dashboard
yaml.subtitle: Bench the Instinct — NBA Player Risk Tool for Coaches & GMs
yaml.description: An interactive load management dashboard predicting which NBA players are at risk of missing significant game time

markdown.markdown:
```{r setup, include=FALSE} knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE) ``` ::: {.hero-banner} ::: {.badge-red} INTERACTIVE TOOL :::
*"We know load caused the spike. Now we know who's next. The question is whether the league acts on data — or waits for the next Tatum."*
Built for **coaches, GMs, and medical staff** who need a data-backed answer to the question they have been asking on instinct for years: *should we rest this player?* ::: --- ## What the Dashboard Does ::: {.grid} ::: {.g-col-4} ::: {.part-card} ### 🦵 The Spike Visual context for why this tool exists. The 2024–25 rupture spike, load trends over time, and the confirmed injury record since 1990. ::: ::: ::: {.g-col-4} ::: {.part-card .red} ### 🔮 Who's Next? Filterable risk table of 2024 NBA players ranked by predicted % of next season's games missed. Restricted to meaningful contributors (≥20 rolling minutes). ::: ::: ::: {.g-col-4} ::: {.part-card} ### ⚡ Bench the Instinct The load simulator. Enter a player's rolling minutes, prior absence history, and career tenure. Get a predicted risk score. Compare scenarios before making the call. ::: ::: ::: --- ## Launch the Dashboard ::: {.text-center style="padding: 40px 0;"} [🏀 Open Full Dashboard](https://7tmnpt-keziah-vickraman.shinyapps.io/nba-achilles/){.btn .btn-danger .btn-lg target="_blank" style="font-size: 1.2rem; padding: 16px 40px; margin-right: 16px;"} [📄 Download Academic Paper](submission_final_042026.pdf){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg target="_blank" style="font-size: 1.2rem; padding: 16px 40px;"} ::: --- ## In-Page Preview {#preview} ::: {.callout-tip} ## Tip The embedded preview below may take 10–15 seconds to load. For the best experience use the **Open Full Dashboard** button above. ::: ```{=html}
🏀 Bench the Instinct — NBA Risk Dashboard Powered by R Shiny
``` --- ## Model at a Glance ```{r model-summary} #| label: tbl-model-summary #| tbl-cap: "Model performance on held-out test set (2022–2025)" tibble::tribble( ~Metric, ~Value, ~`Plain English`, "R²", "0.747", "Explains 74.7% of variance in games missed", "RMSE", "0.133", "Average prediction error ~11 games on 82-game scale", "MAE", "0.100", "Median prediction error ~8 games on 82-game scale" ) |> knitr::kable(align = c("l", "r", "l")) ``` ::: {.callout-important} ## Honest limitations `pct_games_missed` captures **all** absence reasons — Achilles injuries, other injuries, rest decisions, and suspensions. The model predicts load-related game unavailability as a proxy for injury burden. It does not diagnose specific injury types. `back_to_back_games` and `total_minutes` were removed from the model after PDP analysis revealed survivorship bias — healthy players accumulate more of both, so the features had inverted relationships with the outcome. The primary load predictor is `avg_rolling_minutes_3` (lagged 3-game rolling average minutes), which correctly shows a positive monotonic relationship with games missed. Full methodology in the [academic paper](submission.qmd). ::: --- ## Who Should Use This | Role | How to use it | |---|---| | **Head coach** | Run the what-if simulator before a heavy road trip — see how reduced minutes changes predicted risk | | **GM / front office** | Filter the risk table before contract decisions — who is likely to miss 20+ games next season? | | **Medical / performance staff** | Flag high-risk players for targeted prehabilitation or biomechanical screening | | **League operations** | Aggregate risk scores by team to identify scheduling interventions | --- *Dashboard built in R Shiny · Model: XGBoost regression · Data: hoopR (2018–2025)* *Keziah Vickraman · Capstone 2025*
